summary: This is an extremely brief post consisting of a single Windows UI tip. Joel shares that pressing Ctrl+C while a Windows popup error message box is displayed copies its text to the clipboard. There is no argument or analysis — it is purely a quick productivity tip shared with readers.
key_points: Ctrl+C copies the text content of Windows popup error message boxes to the clipboard
This is a hidden/lesser-known Windows UI feature that most users don't discover on their own
The post demonstrates Joel's habit of sharing small, practical tips alongside longer essays
money_quotes: Did you know… you can Ctrl+C to copy the text of a Windows popup error message box to the clipboard?
key_insight: Windows has a hidden feature where Ctrl+C copies the text of popup error dialogs to the clipboard, useful for reporting or searching for error messages.
